description_long: This office building features a beige facade with three visible stories, each lined with evenly spaced rectangular windows that have white frames and sills. The roof is steeply pitched and gray, with a series of small dormer windows on one side, suggesting attic or loft space. The main entrance is a single, arched doorway with a brown door, centrally located on the narrower face of the building. The longer facade includes a set of double doors, likely serving as a secondary or main entrance, and is also adorned with regularly spaced windows. The building's overall shape is a rectangular prism with a gabled roof, and the exterior is simple, with minimal ornamentation. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from masonry or concrete, typical of traditional European office buildings.
description: A beige, three-story office building with a steep gray gabled roof, regular white-framed windows, and arched and double-door entrances.
description_view_0: The rear or side view shows a plain beige wall with no windows or doors, and the sloped gray roof is visible.
description_view_1: The narrow facade features an arched main entrance and three rows of white-framed windows, with the pitched roof above.
description_view_2: The long side displays a series of evenly spaced windows and a set of double doors, with the full expanse of the gabled roof visible.
description_view_3: The opposite narrow end is plain, with no windows or doors, and the roof's slope is clearly seen.
attribute_window_pattern: Regularly spaced rectangular windows in horizontal rows on each floor, with symmetrical alignment and repetition.
